title: Why Routing?
description: Understand client‑side routing and the need for React Router.

slides.title: From Multi‑Page to Single‑Page 🧭
slides.content:
# Traditional vs Single‑Page Apps

In traditional websites, every time you click a link, the browser requests a new HTML page from the server. The page refreshes – slow and jarring.

**Single‑Page Applications (SPAs)** load a single HTML page and dynamically update content as the user interacts. This feels faster and smoother.

But how do we handle different URLs (like `/about`, `/profile`) without a page refresh? That's where **client‑side routing** comes in.

## React Router

React Router is the most popular routing library for React. It lets you map different URLs to different components, all without a full page reload.

It also keeps the UI in sync with the URL.

quizData.question: What is the main benefit of client‑side routing in a SPA?

options.text: It makes the app load faster initially
It allows navigation without a full page refresh
It replaces the need for a backend

quizData.explanation: Client‑side routing updates the UI based on the URL without requesting a new HTML document from the server.
